tasks.title: Add ropey dependency to Cargo.toml
tasks.description: Add ropey = '1.6' dependency as specified in PRD for efficient text storage
tasks.details: Edit Cargo.toml: add `ropey = "1.6"` under [dependencies]. Run `cargo check` to verify dependency resolution. No other changes needed.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `cargo build` and `cargo check` to confirm dependency resolves without errors. Verify no version conflicts.

tasks.title: Create TextBuffer module with ropey::Rope wrapper
tasks.description: Implement src/editor/buffer.rs with core editing operations using ropey::Rope
tasks.details: Create src/editor/buffer.rs. Implement TextBuffer struct wrapping Rope. Add methods: new(), from_string(content: &str), insert(pos: usize, text: &str), remove(pos: usize, len: usize), line(line_idx: usize) -> &str, line_count() -> usize, line_to_char(line_idx: usize) -> usize, char_to_line(char_idx: usize) -> usize, len() -> usize. Use Rope::insert, Rope::remove, Rope::get_line, etc.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: create buffer from 4MB string, insert/delete 1KB chunks at random positions, verify line_count and line_to_char accuracy, measure memory usage <50MB for 4MB file.

tasks.title: Implement EditHistory with operation-based undo/redo
tasks.description: Create src/editor/history.rs with EditOperation enum and rope-native undo/redo stacks
tasks.details: Create src/editor/history.rs. Define enum EditOperation { Insert { pos: usize, text: RopeSlice }, Delete { pos: usize, text: RopeSlice } }. Implement EditHistory { undo_stack: Vec<EditOperation>, redo_stack: Vec<EditOperation> }. Methods: record_operation(op), undo(buffer: &mut TextBuffer), redo(buffer: &mut TextBuffer). Group operations with 500ms debounce timer using std::time::Instant.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: 100 insert/delete/undo/redo sequences on 1MB buffer, verify content matches original after full undo/redo cycle, test operation grouping doesn't merge across 500ms boundaries.

tasks.title: Create ViewState for Phase 1 virtual scrolling (no wrap)
tasks.description: Implement src/editor/view.rs tracking first visible line and scroll state for horizontal scrolling
tasks.details: Create src/editor/view.rs. ViewState { first_visible_line: usize, scroll_offset_y: f32, viewport_height: f32, line_height: f32, horizontal_scroll: f32 }. Methods: update_viewport(height: f32), get_visible_line_range() -> (usize, usize) with 5-line overscan, scroll_to_line(line: usize), pixel_to_line(pixel_y: f32) -> usize.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: simulate viewport heights 100-2000px, verify visible range calculation with overscan, test scroll_to_line positions cursor correctly.

tasks.title: Implement LineCache with LRU eviction for Phase 1
tasks.description: Create src/editor/line_cache.rs caching per-line galleys with content hash keys
tasks.details: Create src/editor/line_cache.rs. LineCache with HashMap<u64, (Galley, f32)> where key = hash(line_content). LRU eviction max 200 entries using lru crate or manual VecDeque. Methods: get_galley(line_content: &str, max_width: f32, painter: &Painter) -> Galley, invalidate(). Single-line galleys (no wrap).
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: cache 200 lines then add 201st verifies LRU eviction, galley content hash matches input, galley.measure().size correct for various line lengths.

tasks.title: Create basic FerriteEditor widget skeleton
tasks.description: Implement src/editor/ferrite_editor.rs main widget structure integrating all Phase 1 modules
tasks.details: Create src/editor/ferrite_editor.rs. FerriteEditor { buffer: TextBuffer, history: EditHistory, view: ViewState, line_cache: LineCache, cursor: Cursor }. Implement ui(ctx: &Context, ui: &mut Ui) -> Response. Basic paint loop: clear cache on content change, render visible lines + gutter.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: create widget, verify ui() returns Response, no panics on empty buffer.

tasks.title: Implement Phase 1 basic rendering with horizontal scroll
tasks.description: Render visible lines using egui::Painter with line numbers and horizontal scrolling
tasks.details: In FerriteEditor::ui(): get visible_range = view.get_visible_line_range(). For each line: galley = line_cache.get_galley(buffer.line(i), ui.available_width()). painter.galley at correct position with horizontal_scroll offset. Reuse existing line_numbers.rs for gutter. Fixed line_height = 20.0.
tasks.testStrategy: Visual test: load 4MB file with long lines, verify only ~20 lines rendered (not 100k), horizontal scroll works, line numbers align.

tasks.title: Add Phase 1 basic input handling
tasks.description: Implement character insertion, cursor movement, backspace/delete, arrow keys, home/end
tasks.details: Create src/editor/input.rs. InputHandler methods: handle_event(buffer: &mut TextBuffer, event: &Event). Implement: typing inserts at cursor, backspace/delete, arrow keys update cursor with char_to_line/line_to_char, Home/End (line/document), PageUp/Down via view.scroll. Single cursor { line: usize, col: usize } converted to char index.
tasks.testStrategy: Manual test: type 100 chars, navigate with arrows/Home/End/Page, verify cursor position via debug print, backspace works.

tasks.title: Integrate FerriteEditor into EditorWidget with feature flag
tasks.description: Modify src/editor/widget.rs to use FerriteEditor behind feature flag, preserve Tab state compatibility
tasks.details: In src/editor/widget.rs: if cfg!(feature = "ferrite-editor") { FerriteEditor::new(tab) } else { TextEdit }. Preserve cursor position and scroll offset when switching. Add debounced sync: every 500ms or on save/tab-switch, tab.content = buffer.sync_to_string(). For files >5MB, disable live preview.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: load file, toggle feature flag, verify editor switches without losing cursor position, debounced sync triggers correctly.

tasks.title: Phase 1 validation: memory testing and basic functionality
tasks.description: Test Phase 1 success criteria: 4MB file <50MB memory, basic editing, horizontal scroll
tasks.details: Create test script: load 1KB/100KB/1MB/4MB files, measure RSS via /proc or Task Manager, test typing/scrolling/undo. Verify live preview sync for <5MB, disabled message for >5MB.
tasks.testStrategy: Automated benchmarks + manual verification. Success: 4MB file uses <50MB RAM, all Phase 1 input works fluidly.

tasks.title: Implement Phase 2 word wrap support
tasks.description: Add word wrap: track visual rows vs logical lines, update ViewState and LineCache
tasks.details: Extend ViewState: track visual rows, calculate wrapped line heights. LineCache: store multi-galley wrapped lines using LayoutJob with max_width. Cursor navigation: visual up/down respects wraps. Scrollbar: total_height = sum(wrapped_line_heights). Integrate max line width and Zen mode centering.
tasks.testStrategy: Test long wrapped lines (100+ chars), verify visual navigation matches TextEdit, scrollbar thumb correct size.

tasks.title: Add Phase 2 selection and clipboard support
tasks.description: Implement shift+arrow selection, click-drag, Ctrl+A/C/X/V, double/triple click
tasks.details: Extend cursor to Selection { anchor: CursorPos, head: CursorPos }. Render selection background before galleys. Clipboard: egui::Context::output_mut().copied_text. Wrap-aware selection endpoints. Double-click: word boundaries via char classification, triple-click: line.
tasks.testStrategy: Test all selection modes match TextEdit behavior exactly, copy/paste 1MB selections without memory spikes.

tasks.title: Port Phase 2 syntax highlighting to per-line cache
tasks.description: Adapt existing layouter logic to highlight only visible lines, cache highlighted galleys
tasks.details: In LineCache::get_galley(): if syntax enabled, create LayoutJob with existing syntax layouter. Cache includes theme+highlight state in hash key. Only process visible_range lines.
tasks.testStrategy: Load syntax-highlighted file, verify colors match existing editor, performance same or better for 4MB files.

tasks.title: Implement Phase 2 search highlights and bracket matching
tasks.description: Port search match highlighting and bracket matching to FerriteEditor
tasks.details: In rendering: overlay search matches with distinct color (current match brighter). Bracket matching: parse visible lines +1 for pairs, underline matching brackets. Scroll-to-match via view.scroll_to_line().
tasks.testStrategy: Search test: find/replace in 4MB file, verify highlights only on visible lines, scroll-to-match works.

tasks.title: Add Phase 2 find & replace integration
tasks.description: Implement selection-based find, replace at cursor, replace all
tasks.details: Extend InputHandler: Ctrl+F find, Ctrl+H replace. Replace operations recorded in EditHistory. Replace all: batch operations with undo grouping.
tasks.testStrategy: Test replace all 1000 occurrences in 1MB file completes <2s, single undo restores original.

tasks.title: Phase 2 validation: feature parity testing
tasks.description: Verify word wrap, selection, syntax, search work without regressions vs TextEdit
tasks.details: Side-by-side comparison with TextEdit: identical behavior for all keyboard shortcuts, visual appearance, performance.
tasks.testStrategy: Manual parity checklist covering all EditorWidget features. Success: no user-visible differences.

tasks.title: Implement Phase 3 IME/CJK support (RELEASE BLOCKER)
tasks.description: Handle egui Event::Ime for composition window, preedit rendering, commit
tasks.details: In InputHandler::handle_event(): match Event::Ime(ime) { Preedit(text) => show underlined composition at cursor, Commit(text) => buffer.insert(cursor, text) }. Position composition window using cursor galley rect. Test Chinese Pinyin, Japanese Romaji, Korean Hangul.
tasks.testStrategy: Manual IME testing on Windows/macOS: input Chinese/Japanese/Korean phrases, verify composition→commit works, mixed CJK/Latin correct.

tasks.title: Add Phase 3 multi-cursor and code folding
tasks.description: Implement multi-cursor editing and fold state integration with hidden rendering
tasks.details: Extend Selection to Vec<CursorPos>. Apply edits simultaneously with offset adjustment. Folding: skip rendering folded regions, adjust view metrics, cursor navigation skips folds.
tasks.testStrategy: Test multi-cursor typing/deletion, fold 100-line region verifies hidden from view/scrol

tasks.title: Phase 3 cleanup: remove TextEdit feature flag
tasks.description: Remove all TextEdit fallback code, make FerriteEditor the only editor
tasks.details: Delete feature flag from Cargo.toml, src/editor/widget.rs, all conditional paths. Update docs. Final memory/performance validation.
tasks.testStrategy: Full regression test suite, verify no TextEdit strings remain in binary (strings command), 10MB file <20MB RAM.

tasks.title: Setup SignPath.io code signing for Windows releases
tasks.description: Integrate SignPath into GitHub Actions release workflow
tasks.details: Create SignPath.io account. Modify .github/workflows/release.yml: add SignPath signing step after Windows exe build. Create docs/technical/platform/code-signing.md with setup instructions. Test signature verification.
tasks.testStrategy: Trigger release workflow, verify .exe signed (sigcheck), Windows Defender no false positives, SmartScreen shows verified.

tasks.title: Export new editor modules in src/editor/mod.rs
tasks.description: Add pub mod declarations for new modules to editor public API
tasks.details: In src/editor/mod.rs: pub mod ferrite_editor; pub mod buffer; pub mod history; etc. Re-export key types: pub use ferrite_editor::FerriteEditor; pub use buffer::TextBuffer;
tasks.testStrategy: cargo check after integration, verify public API compiles.

tasks.title: Add large file preview disablement (>5MB)
tasks.description: Show 'Large file - preview disabled' message for files >5MB
tasks.details: In EditorWidget: if buffer.len() > 5_000_000 { ui.label("Large file - preview disabled"); return; } else { sync preview }. Update split view right pane accordingly.
tasks.testStrategy: Test 4MB (preview works), 6MB (message shows, no sync attempts).

tasks.title: Connect Undo/Redo to FerriteEditor Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y Shortcuts
tasks.description: Integrate EditHistory undo/redo functionality with Ctrl+Z and Ctrl+Y keyboard shortcuts in FerriteEditor's ui() method, ensuring buffer state and cursor position are correctly updated after operations.
tasks.details: In src/editor/ferrite_editor.rs::ui(ctx: &Context, ui: &mut Ui) -> Response: 1) Use ui.input(|i| { if i.key_pressed(egui::Key::Z) && i.modifiers.ctrl { if history.can_undo() { history.undo(&mut buffer); view.invalidate_cache(); cursor.restore_from_history_state(); } } if i.key_pressed(egui::Key::Y) && i.modifiers.ctrl { if history.can_redo() { history.redo(&mut buffer); view.invalidate_cache(); cursor.restore_from_history_state(); } } }) before main input handling. 2) Add cursor state tracking to EditHistory::record_operation() by storing CursorPos { line: usize, col: usize } in EditOperation enum variant. 3) Implement restore_from_history_state() on Cursor using last recorded cursor pos from history.undo_stack.top() or history.redo_stack.top(). 4) After undo/redo, call line_cache.invalidate(), view.update_viewport(ui.available_height()), and ensure selection state is cleared or restored. 5) Handle edge cases: no-op if !can_undo/can_redo, preserve horizontal_scroll, test with grouped operations from Task 3 debounce. 6) On macOS, also support Cmd+Z/Cmd+Y via i.modifiers.command.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test: Create FerriteEditor, perform 5 insert/delete ops, verify Ctrl+Z undoes to original state (buffer.content(), cursor.line/col, line_cache invalidated), Ctrl+Y redoes correctly; test can_undo/can_redo prevents extra calls. Integration test: Load 1MB file, do 10 undo/redo cycles via simulated key events, verify no panics, cursor restores to pre-edit position, rendering updates (visible lines match buffer), measure performance <50ms per undo/redo. Visual test: Type 'hello world', Ctrl+Z, verify text removed and cursor at start of line.

tasks.title: Connect Font Settings to FerriteEditor
tasks.description: Integrate app font settings (family and size) with FerriteEditor, adding font family support, ensuring set_font_size() responds to changes, and invalidating line cache on font updates. Verify and test across different font configurations as Phase 4 integration work.
tasks.details: 1) Locate font settings in Settings panel (likely src/settings.rs or app state: font_family: String, font_size: f32). Add callback/on_change handler: when settings change, call editor.set_font_size(settings.font_size); editor.set_font_family(&settings.font_family) if method exists or implement it. 2) In src/editor/ferrite_editor.rs, replace hardcoded FontId::monospace with dynamic FontId: add fields font_id: FontId, font_size: f32 to FerriteEditor struct. Implement pub fn set_font_size(&mut self, size: f32) { self.font_size = size; self.font_id = ui.fonts(|f| f.fonts_for_font_id(FontId::Proportional).clone().with_size(size)); self.view.invalidate_line_cache(); } and pub fn set_font_family(&mut self, family: &str, ui: &Ui) { let fonts = ui.fonts(); let font_id = fonts.families().iter().find(|f| f.name == family).map(|f| FontId::new(f.id, self.font_size)); self.font_id = font_id.unwrap_or(FontId::monospace()); self.view.invalidate_line_cache(); } 3) In FerriteEditor::ui(): Pass self.font_id to line_cache.get_galley(line_text, available_width, &self.font_id). Update painter.galley() calls to use dynamic font. 4) In main app loop or Settings::ui(): use ui.context().request_repaint_after(Duration::from_millis(16)) after font change; send message to editor instances via event system or direct ref if single editor. 5) Ensure ViewState::invalidate_line_cache() clears all cached galleys and recomputes line heights/metrics. 6) Handle large files: font changes should not trigger full reparse, only visual cache invalidation.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: 1) Create FerriteEditor, call set_font_size(14.0), verify line_cache.galley uses FontId with size 14.0, cache invalidates (len==0 post-invalidation). 2) Test set_font_family("Courier New", ui), verify FontId matches family. Integration tests: 1) Load 1MB file, change settings font_size 12->18, verify visual re-render with new size, line heights update, no content change. 2) Switch font_family monospace->serif, verify galleys use new font (measure galley.size().x differs). 3) Large file 10MB: font change <100ms, verify cache rebuilds incrementally. 4) Manual: Settings panel → change font → immediate visual update in FerriteEditor, scroll/selection preserved. 5) Regression: verify Ctrl+Z/Y, word wrap, selection unchanged by font.

tasks.title: Connect Format Toolbar to FerriteEditor
tasks.description: Integrate the format toolbar buttons (Bold, Italic, Code, Link, Headings, etc.) with FerriteEditor by updating handlers to use buffer operations for markdown formatting insertion and selection wrapping as Phase 4 integration work.
tasks.details: 1) Locate toolbar button handlers in ui/ribbon.rs or equivalent UI component (search for button callbacks like 'bold', 'italic', 'format_bold'). Current handlers likely call TextEdit::perform_xx() methods. 2) Refactor handlers to access active FerriteEditor instance via app state or context (e.g., app_state.active_tab_mut().editor_mut()). 3) In src/editor/ferrite_editor.rs, add public helper methods: pub fn insert_markdown_at_cursor(&mut self, fmt: &str) { let pos = self.cursor.pos(); self.buffer.insert(pos, fmt); self.history.push(); self.invalidate_cache(); self.cursor.move_right(fmt.len()); } pub fn wrap_selection(&mut self, left: &str, right: &str) { if let Some(sel) = self.selection { let (start, end) = self.buffer.selection_range(sel); self.buffer.insert(start, left); self.buffer.insert(end + left.len(), right); self.history.push(); self.invalidate_cache(); self.cursor.set_pos(end + left.len()); } else { self.insert_markdown_at_cursor(&format!("{}{}", left, right)); } } 4) Map toolbar buttons to markdown patterns: Bold: "**" "**", Italic: "*" "*", Code: "`" "`", Link: detect URL or prompt input then "[text](url)" at cursor/selection, Headings: "# " repeated by level at line start. 5) Handle edge cases: empty selection → insert pair at cursor, non-empty → wrap, preserve cursor position post-operation, multi-cursor if implemented (Task 18). 6) Update keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+B/I/K) to call same methods via FerriteEditor::ui() input handling.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: 1) Create FerriteEditor, position cursor, call insert_markdown_at_cursor("**text**"), verify buffer.content() contains markdown, cursor after closing **, cache invalidated. 2) Select text "hello", call wrap_selection("**", "**"), verify "**hello**". 3) Test heading insertion at line start. Integration tests: 1) Load sample.md, click toolbar Bold/Italic/Link buttons with/without selection, verify markdown inserted correctly, undo/redo works (Task 23), cursor position preserved. 2) Test all 8+ format buttons across cursor/selection cases. 3) Load 1MB file, verify formatting ops don't cause lag (>60fps), no panics on edge cases (empty buffer, line ends). Visual test: toolbar buttons highlight active state (bold text when **selection**).

tasks.title: Connect Outline Panel and Minimap to FerriteEditor
tasks.description: Integrate the outline panel and minimap with FerriteEditor for bidirectional synchronization, clickable navigation using scroll_to_line() or ensure_line_visible(), and optional search highlight activation as Phase 4 integration work.
tasks.details: 1) Locate outline panel in ui/outline_panel.rs: find click handlers for heading items (likely on egui::Button or selectable_label clicked), refactor to access active FerriteEditor via app_state.active_tab_mut().editor_mut(), call editor.scroll_to_line(heading_line_idx, ensure_visible: true) or editor.ensure_line_visible(heading_line_idx). Add optional highlight: if ui.ctrl(), call editor.highlight_line_range(heading_line_idx, heading_line_idx+1, SearchHighlight::Yellow). 2) Locate minimap in editor/minimap.rs: update click handler in minimap.ui() - convert pixel_y click position to target_line = self.pixel_to_line(click_y), call editor.scroll_to_line(target_line). Ensure minimap canvas resizes with editor viewport. 3) Bidirectional sync: In FerriteEditor::ui(), after view.update(), emit events or set app_state.editor_first_visible_line = self.view.first_visible_line, app_state.editor_top_line = self.view.scroll_offset_y / self.view.line_height. In outline_panel.update() and minimap.update(), read app_state.editor_first_visible_line to set scroll position/highlighted range (e.g., outline scroll to make editor_top_line visible, minimap scroll thumb to match). 4) Add to FerriteEditor public API if missing: pub fn scroll_to_line(&mut self, line: usize, ensure_visible: bool), pub fn ensure_line_visible(&mut self, line: usize), pub fn highlight_line_range(&mut self, start_line: usize, end_line: usize, color: egui::Color32), pub fn get_first_visible_line(&self) -> usize. 5) Handle large files: throttle sync updates to 60fps using egui::Frame::none().request_repaint_after(Duration::from_millis(16)). Invalidate minimap cache on editor scroll/content change.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: 1) Mock FerriteEditor, call scroll_to_line(100), verify view.first_visible_line ≈100, scroll_offset_y positions line 100 at top. 2) Test ensure_line_visible(50) with first_visible=40, verify scrolls minimally to make 50 visible. 3) Test highlight_line_range(10,12,Yellow), verify painter.rect_filled highlights lines 10-12. Integration tests: 1) Load 1MB markdown with 50 headings, click outline heading #20, verify editor scrolls to line 500±5, optional Ctrl+click shows yellow highlight. 2) Drag editor scroll to line 300, verify outline selection moves to nearest heading, minimap thumb follows. 3) Click minimap at 75% position, verify jumps to line ≈0.75*total_lines. 4) Performance: scroll 1MB file continuously 30s, verify no >16ms frame times, minimap/outlines update smoothly without lag.

tasks.title: Connect Line Numbers Toggle Setting to FerriteEditor
tasks.description: Integrate the settings panel's line numbers toggle with FerriteEditor to conditionally render the line number gutter, dynamically recalculate gutter width on toggle, and ensure the text area expands/contracts without requiring app restart as Phase 4 integration work.
tasks.details: 1) Locate line numbers setting in settings panel (likely src/settings.rs or app_state: show_line_numbers: bool, accessed via Ctrl+, or gear icon). Add on_change callback: when toggled, access active FerriteEditor via app_state.active_tab_mut().editor_mut(), call editor.set_show_line_numbers(new_value), triggering immediate ui repaint. 2) In src/editor/ferrite_editor.rs, add field show_line_numbers: bool to FerriteEditor struct (default: true). Implement pub fn set_show_line_numbers(&mut self, show: bool) { self.show_line_numbers = show; self.line_cache.clear(); self.view.request_repaint(); } 3) In FerriteEditor::ui(): calculate gutter_width = if self.show_line_numbers { self.calculate_gutter_width(ui.available_width()) } else { 0.0 }; reserve space: ui.allocate_exact_size(egui::vec2(gutter_width, ui.available_height()), egui::Sense::drag()).response; if self.show_line_numbers { paint_line_numbers_gutter(ui.painter().clone(), self.view, self.line_cache.num_lines(), gutter_width); } 4) Implement fn calculate_gutter_width(&self, content_width: f32) -> f32 { let max_line_num = self.buffer.num_lines().max(999); let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_no_wrap(format!("{max_line_num}"), f32::INFINITY, &TextStyle::Monospace.into())); galley.size().x + 8.0 } where ui is passed or cached. 5) In main paint loop, offset content rect by (gutter_width, 0.0), ensure horizontal scroll bar adjusts to new content_width = available_width - gutter_width. 6) Handle dynamic resize: on settings change or window resize, invalidate line_cache and request_repaint() to recalculate all galleys with correct available_width.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: 1) Create FerriteEditor, call set_show_line_numbers(false), verify gutter_width==0.0, no line numbers painted in ui() mock. 2) Load 1000-line buffer, toggle true/false, verify calculate_gutter_width() returns ~40px for 4-digit max, content rect x_offset matches. 3) Test cache invalidation: toggle off->on, verify line_cache.len()==0 post-toggle. Integration tests: 1) Open settings (Ctrl+,), toggle line numbers with 1MB file loaded, verify gutter appears/disappears instantly, text area expands/contracts (measure rect widths before/after), no restart needed. 2) Test with long lines: toggle on, verify horizontal scroll bar adjusts to content_width - gutter_width. 3) Resize window while toggled, verify gutter width recalculates for new line count display. 4) Verify persistence: toggle off, switch tabs, return, setting remains off.

tasks.title: Verify File Save and Encoding Sync with FerriteEditor
tasks.description: Verify FerriteEditor integration handles file loading/saving across multiple encodings correctly, including round-trip editing, BOM handling, and unsaved changes indicators as Phase 4 integration verification.
tasks.details: 1) Locate file I/O logic in files/mod.rs, src/editor/widget.rs, or src/app_state/tabs.rs - search for 'std::fs::read_to_string', 'File::open', 'buffer.from_string', or 'tab.content = buffer.sync_to_string()'. 2) Test loading: Create test files in UTF-8 (with/without BOM), UTF-16LE/BE, ISO-8859-1/Latin-1, Windows-1252 with accented chars (é, ñ, ü). Verify buffer.to_string() displays correct content via egui galley rendering. 3) Verify save sync: In EditorWidget::ui() or tab save handler, confirm buffer.to_string() → file write preserves original content (no double-conversion). Check for encoding param in File::write (should use UTF-8 output). 4) Round-trip test: Load UTF-16 file → edit (insert/delete) → save → reload → verify content/selection identical. 5) Edge cases: BOM stripping on load, mixed-encoding detection failure fallback to UTF-8, invalid UTF-8 sequences (replace with �), zero-byte files, 10MB+ files. 6) Unsaved changes: Verify * indicator in tab title updates on buffer changes, clears on save. Add debug logging: println!("Loaded {} with encoding {:?}, buffer len: {}", path, detected_encoding, buffer.len()). If no explicit encoding detection exists, note limitation and create follow-up task.
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Create 10 test files (UTF-8/BOM, UTF-16LE/BE, Latin1, Shift-JIS, 4KB + 4MB sizes) with known content ("Hello café naïve 🚀"). 2) Manual test suite: Load each → verify visual content matches expected → edit 3 lines → save → reload → diff content (use external diff tool or checksum). 3) Round-trip verification: Edit → save → reload 5x, confirm no corruption. 4) Edge case tests: Load invalid UTF-8 → verify graceful handling (no panic), BOM file → verify stripped, unsaved indicator → type → see *, save → * disappears. 5) Performance: Load/save 10MB file <3s. 6) Cross-platform: Test on Windows/Linux file paths. Success criteria: 100% round-trip accuracy across 5 encodings, no panics, unsaved indicator works.

tasks.title: Implement Editor-Preview Scroll Synchronization
tasks.description: Add bidirectional scroll sync between FerriteEditor (left) and Markdown preview (right) in split view, updating the old TextEdit-based implementation for the new FerriteEditor.
tasks.details: Create/update src/preview/sync_scroll.rs with ScrollSync struct managing synchronization state. Implement: 1) From FerriteEditor::ViewState, expose first_visible_line, scroll_offset_y, total_content_height (sum of wrapped line heights from LineCache), viewport_height. 2) In preview, use pulldown-cmark or similar to generate HTML, render via egui::ScrollArea capturing scroll_delta and content_height. 3) Bidirectional sync: on editor scroll change, compute scroll_percentage = (first_visible_line * avg_line_height + scroll_offset_y) / total_height, apply same percentage to preview ScrollArea.scroll_to_y(percentage * preview_height). Reverse for preview→editor using view.pixel_to_line(). 4) Handle edge cases: debounce scroll events (50ms), skip sync during programmatic scrolls, account for wrapped lines by using visual row metrics, clamp percentages 0.0-1.0. 5) Add ui checkbox 'Sync Scrolling' in toolbar, gated by split view mode. Use egui::ScrollArea::auto_shrink(false) on both panes. Integrate into main app ui loop: if sync_enabled && split_mode, call sync.update(&editor.view, &preview_scroll).
tasks.testStrategy: Manual verification: 1) Open 1MB markdown file in split view, scroll editor→verify preview follows instantly (<100ms lag), reverse direction. 2) Test long wrapped lines (200+ chars), code blocks, tables—verify alignment of corresponding content. 3) Edge cases: scroll to top/bottom, partial viewport content, disable/enable sync mid-scroll. 4) Performance: scroll rapidly through 10k-line file, no jank (>30fps), memory stable. 5) Toggle sync off/on, verify independent scrolling. Debug print scroll percentages match ±0.01.

tasks.title: Fix Markdown Preview Panel Alignment Issues
tasks.description: Standardize horizontal margins, vertical spacing between block elements, and list item indentation in the rendered Markdown preview panel to ensure consistent visual alignment across paragraphs, lists, formatted text, and other elements.
tasks.details: 1) Locate Markdown preview rendering in src/preview/sync_scroll.rs or src/preview/mod.rs - search for pulldown-cmark usage, egui::ScrollArea, or HTML/CSS injection points. 2) Implement consistent CSS styling: Create a style struct or inject via egui::Style::text_styles and egui::Visuals. Set base left margin: all block elements (p, h1-h6, ul, ol, blockquote, pre, table) use margin-left: 0.5em, padding-left: 0. 3) Vertical spacing: Standardize margin-bottom: 1em for all block elements except last child (use :last-child { margin-bottom: 0 }), line-height: 1.5em for paragraphs. 4) List indentation: ul/ol { margin-left: 2em, padding-left: 0.5em }, li { margin-bottom: 0.25em }. 5) Inline elements (strong, em, code): Ensure consistent baseline alignment via vertical-align: baseline, no extra margins. 6) Test with complex markdown: nested lists, code blocks, tables, headings - verify all elements align to same left baseline. 7) Integrate with ScrollSync: Ensure content_height calculations account for standardized spacing, invalidate preview cache on style changes. 8) Responsive: Scale margins proportionally for different font sizes via em units.
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Create test markdown file with mixed content: 3 paragraphs, nested bullet/numbered lists (3 levels), H1-H3 headings, bold/italic/code spans, blockquote, code block, table. 2) Load in split view, verify: a) All block elements start at identical left position (measure pixel offset from preview pane edge), b) Consistent 1em vertical gaps between blocks (no overlapping or excessive spacing), c) List items indented exactly 2em from paragraph left edge. 3) Toggle font sizes (12px, 16px, 20px) via settings - verify proportional scaling. 4) Scroll sync test: Scroll to list item in editor, verify preview shows correctly aligned corresponding content. 5) Edge cases: Empty lines between blocks, trailing whitespace, single-line vs multi-line paragraphs.

tasks.title: Implement Rendered View Source Line Tracking for Scroll Sync
tasks.description: Replace mathematical position calculations with metadata-based source line tracking by attaching invisible line number data to rendered markdown elements and querying visible viewport content for bidirectional scroll synchronization.
tasks.details: Create src/preview/line_tracking.rs with RenderedLineTracker struct. 1) During markdown rendering (using pulldown-cmark or existing HTML generation), attach invisible metadata to each rendered element: add `data-source-line` attribute to block elements (p, h1-h6, ul/ol/li, code blocks, tables) mapping to exact source line number from TextBuffer. For nested content like table rows or list items, propagate line number to child elements (tr, li). Use egui::Label or custom widget with `.data_source_line(line_idx)` or similar. 2) Implement `query_visible_lines(viewport_top: f32, viewport_bottom: f32) -> Option<(source_line_top: usize, offset_percent: f32)>` by iterating rendered elements in viewport, finding first/last with data-source-line attributes, calculating precise top-of-viewport source line using element rects and offsets. 3) Expose public API: `get_top_visible_source_line() -> usize` and `scroll_to_source_line(target_line: usize) -> f32` pixel offset. 4) Cache line-to-element mappings in ScrollSync for O(1) lookups during frequent scroll events. 5) Handle edge cases: empty lines skipped, multi-line elements get primary source line, content changes invalidate cache via buffer len check. 6) Integrate as prerequisite into src/preview/sync_scroll.rs Task 29 by replacing scroll_percentage calculations with tracker queries.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: 1) Render 100-line markdown with mixed content (headers, lists, code blocks, tables), verify all block elements have correct data-source-line attributes matching TextBuffer lines. 2) Simulate viewport queries: mock egui rects, verify query_visible_lines returns correct top source line and offset for various scroll positions (top, middle, bottom of elements). 3) Integration test: load 1MB markdown in split view, scroll editor to line 50000→verify preview query_visible_lines returns ~50000, reverse direction. 4) Performance: 1000 query_visible_lines calls on 10k rendered elements completes <1ms average. 5) Edge cases: scroll to empty line, multi-line tables, folded regions (if applicable), cache invalidation on buffer insert/delete.

tasks.title: Fix Search Highlighting Accuracy with Word Wrap Enabled
tasks.description: Resolve misalignment of search highlight positions on large files when word wrap is enabled by fixing the highlight positioning calculation to properly account for wrapped lines and verifying accuracy across all file types.
tasks.details: 1) Locate search highlighting implementation from Task 14 in src/editor/ferrite_editor.rs or src/editor/search.rs - search for 'search matches', 'highlight', 'overlay', or 'current match brighter'. Identify highlight position calculation logic (likely using buffer byte offsets or line/column positions). 2) Analyze word wrap integration: In FerriteEditor::ui(), find LineCache or wrapped line height calculations, verify get_char_position_on_screen() or similar accounts for wrap-induced visual offsets. 3) Fix positioning: Modify highlight rendering to map search matches from source buffer positions to wrapped visual coordinates - for each match (line, col_start, col_end), compute wrapped line segments using LineCache::get_wrapped_line_segments(line), then calculate precise egui::Rect for each segment (x = gutter_width + char_width * wrapped_col_offset, y = line_top + segment_line_offset * line_height). Use egui_painter.rect_filled() with rounded corners for multi-segment highlights. 4) Large file optimization: Add heuristic - if buffer.len() > 4MB or line_count > 10000, show warning toast 'Word wrap may slow search highlighting' but do not auto-disable. 5) Cross-file verification: Test highlighting in markdown, code (rust/python), plain text, tables - ensure matches spanning wrapped lines are fully highlighted without gaps/overlap. 6) Edge cases: Handle matches at line ends (split across wrap), very long lines (>500 chars), mixed whitespace/tabs. Update search UI to show 'Wrapped lines: X highlights split' for transparency. Performance: Limit highlights to visible viewport +1 screenful using first_visible_line and viewport_height.
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Create test files: 4MB markdown (long wrapped paragraphs, tables), 2MB rust code (long function lines), 1MB plain text (200-char lines). Enable word wrap. 2) Search 'the' (common word), verify ALL highlights align exactly with matching text pixels (zoom 100-200%, measure with screenshot diff or pixel inspector). 3) Multi-line match: Search phrase spanning 2+ wrapped lines, verify continuous highlight without gaps. 4) Scroll test: Scroll rapidly through 1000-line file, verify highlights update correctly (<200ms lag), no position jumps. 5) Toggle word wrap on/off mid-search, verify highlights reposition instantly. 6) Large file (>4MB): Time search+highlight (<2s), verify no freezes. 7) Cross-file: Load .md, .rs, .txt, .log - search across types, verify consistent accuracy. 8) Regression: Re-run Task 14 test suite (4MB find/replace, scroll-to-match).

tasks.title: Investigate Memory Retention After FerriteEditor Tab Closure
tasks.description: Investigate and resolve memory not being released after closing tabs in FerriteEditor by profiling TextBuffer/Rope, LineCache clearing, and retained references, addressing observed RAM retention from 100MB after large document tab closure.
tasks.details: 1) **Reproduce issue**: Create test scenario - open FerriteEditor with 4MB+ file (use Task 28 test files), monitor RSS via `top`/`htop` or `psutil`, verify RAM ~100MB, close tab, confirm no drop after 10s idle. 2) **TextBuffer/Rope check**: In src/editor/ferrite_editor.rs and src/app_state/tabs.rs, trace tab closure: verify `drop(tab)` calls `TextBuffer::drop()` → `Rope::drop()` (Task 2). Add `println!("Buffer dropped: {} bytes", buffer.len())` in TextBuffer Drop impl, test if fires on tab close. Check for Arc<Buffer> clones in tabs or app_state preventing drop. 3) **LineCache clearing**: In FerriteEditor::drop() or tab closure handler (likely src/app_state/tabs.rs::close_tab()), call `self.line_cache.invalidate(); self.line_cache.clear();` explicitly (Task 5). Verify HashMap/LRU empties via `line_cache.entries().len() == 0` log. Check font changes (Task 24) don't retain old galleys. 4) **Retained references**: Use `cargo install cargo-memmap` or `valgrind --tool=massif ./target/release/ferrite` on Linux: `massif-out-text massif.out.* | less`, identify top allocators (expect ropey::Rope, egui::Galley, HashMap). Search codebase for `Rc/Arc` wrapping FerriteEditor/Buffer (grep -r 'Arc::<' src/), especially in EditorWidget (Task 9) or app_state. Check egui immediate-mode retention: ensure no persistent painter/galley clones outside ui() frame. 5) **Profiling tools**: Add `#[global_allocator]` with `jemallocator` for heap stats, or `valgrind --tool=memcheck --leak-check=full` to detect leaks. Test with 10MB file, measure peak vs post-close RSS delta <5MB expected. 6) **Fix candidates**: a) Tabs retain weak refs: change to Weak<Buffer> where possible. b) LineCache LRU not evicting: force `cache.clear()` on drop. c) ViewState/history retain buffer slices: use Cow<str> or owned strings. d) Debounced sync (Task 9) clones full buffer: cancel pending tasks on close.
tasks.testStrategy: 1) **Baseline measurement**: Before fix - load 4MB file (Task 28 UTF-8 test), screenshot htop RSS=~100MB, close tab, wait 30s, verify RSS stays ~100MB. Open new empty tab, verify no drop. 2) **Component tests**: Unit test TextBuffer: `let mut b = TextBuffer::from_string(large_str); drop(b);` verify no heap growth via jemalloc stats. LineCache: `let mut cache = LineCache::new(); cache.get_galley(long_line, width); drop(cache);` verify HashMap dropped. 3) **Integration test**: Load 4MB file → edit 10 lines → close tab → open new 100KB file → verify RSS drops to ~50MB within 5s (log RSS every 500ms). Repeat 5x with syntax highlighting (Task 13) and word wrap (Task 32). 4) **Valgrind verification**: `valgrind --tool=memcheck --leak-check=full --show-leak-kinds=all ./ferrite test_files/4mb.txt` → close tab → verify 'definitely lost: 0 bytes', 'still reachable: <10KB' (egui internals). 5) **Load test**: Open/close 10x 4MB files sequentially, verify final RSS <60MB, no growth trend. 6) **Edge cases**: Test undo history retention (Task 23), font changes (Task 24), search highlights (Task 32) - close tab mid-operation, verify cleanup.

tasks.title: Fix Box Drawing Characters Displaying as Squares in FerriteEditor
tasks.description: Resolve font rendering issue where Unicode box drawing characters (U+2500–U+257F) appear as square boxes by identifying suitable fallback fonts and updating egui font configuration to ensure proper glyph rendering.
tasks.details:
1) **Identify font families with box drawing support**: Research monospace fonts with U+2500–U+257F glyphs - recommended fonts include 'JetBrains Mono', 'Fira Code', 'Cascadia Code', 'Source Code Pro' (full version), 'DejaVu Sans Mono', 'Noto Sans Mono'. Verify via font tools or browser dev tools[1][3][5][6]. 2) **Locate egui font configuration**: In src/editor/ferrite_editor.rs or src/ui/theme.rs (from Tasks 7,14), find `egui::FontDefinitions::load_default_fonts()` or custom `fonts.add_font()` calls. Identify current monospace font family (likely 'monospace' or 'Courier New'). 3) **Add fallback font chain**: Update monospace font definition with comprehensive fallback chain:
```rust
fonts.families.entry(egui::FontId::proportional(14.0)).or_insert_with(|| {
    vec![
        "JetBrains Mono".to_owned(),
        "Fira Code".to_owned(),
        "Cascadia Code".to_owned(),
        "Source Code Pro".to_owned(),
        "DejaVu Sans Mono".to_owned(),
        "Noto Sans Mono".to_owned(),
        "Courier New".to_owned(),
        "monospace".to_owned(),
    ]
});
```
Apply same chain to `egui::TextStyle::Monospace`. 4) **Test unicode-range if using custom font loading**: If loading TTF/OTF files, ensure `FontData::from_static()` includes box drawing range or use system fonts[5]. 5) **Preserve performance**: Limit to monospace family only (not body text) to avoid layout shifts. Clear font cache after update via `ctx.tessellate()`. 6) **Edge cases**: Test with mixed content (code + box chars), zoomed text, word wrap enabled (Task 32 dependency consideration).
tasks.testStrategy:
1) **Create comprehensive test file**: Generate 4KB test file with all box drawing chars U+2500–U+257F (use [3] character table), mixed with code/text: `┌─── Table ───┐
│ JetBrains   │
└─────────────┘`. Save as 'box-drawing-test.txt'. 2) **Visual verification**: Load in FerriteEditor (Tasks 7,14), verify ALL characters render as proper lines/boxes (not tofu squares) at 100%/150% zoom, word wrap on/off. Screenshot before/after comparison. 3) **Cross-platform test**: Test on Windows/Linux/macOS - verify consistent rendering across systems. 4) **Performance check**: Load 4MB file with 1% box chars, verify no FPS drop (<60fps), no layout jitter. 5) **Regression test**: Verify existing monospace text (code, line numbers from Task 7) unchanged. 6) **Font fallback validation**: Temporarily remove primary font from system, verify fallback chain works.

tasks.title: Fix Low Resolution Windows Start Menu Icon
tasks.description: Fix pixelated/low resolution Ferrite icon in Windows Start Menu by ensuring the .ico file contains all required resolutions (16x16, 32x32, 48x48, 256x256) and is properly embedded during build for v0.2.6 release.
tasks.details: 1) **Verify current .ico file**: Locate assets/ferrite.ico or icons/ferrite.ico. Use ImageMagick `identify -verbose ferrite.ico` or online ICO viewer to list embedded sizes. Confirm presence of 16x16, 24x24, 32x32, 48x48, 256x256 PNGs (square, no transparency issues)[1][4]. 2) **Check build configuration**: In build.rs, Cargo.toml [package.metadata.resources], or winres (if used), verify `icons/ferrite.ico` is included: e.g., `winres::WindowsResource::new().set_icon("assets/ferrite.ico")`. Run `cargo build --release` and check target/release/ferrite.exe with Resource Hacker or `identify` to confirm embedded sizes[1]. 3) **Regenerate .ico file**: If missing sizes, use ImageMagick: `convert ferrite-16.png ferrite-32.png ferrite-48.png ferrite-256.png ferrite.ico` ensuring all are PNG with alpha transparency. Recommended sizes per MS docs: minimum 16,24,32,48,256px for Start Menu (32px base, 256px for pins/scaling)[1][3]. Place in assets/ with clear naming. 4) **Update build script**: Ensure build.rs copies high-res .ico: `embed_resource::compile("assets/ferrite.rc", embed_resource::CompileMode::BuildScript)` where ferrite.rc contains `1 ICON "ferrite.ico"`. 5) **Version tagging**: Update for v0.2.6 in Cargo.toml [package.version = "0.2.6"].
tasks.testStrategy: 1) **Build verification**: `cargo build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc`, use Resource Hacker or `gresource extract target/release/ferrite.exe /microsoft/windows/icon/1/256` to confirm 16/32/48/256px icons embedded. 2) **Visual testing matrix**: Install on Windows 11/10: a) Pin to Start Menu → verify crisp 32px icon; b) Taskbar pin → verify 24px/48px scaling; c) Alt+Tab switcher → verify no pixelation; d) Test 100%/125%/150% DPI scaling → confirm Windows uses exact matches not upscaled 256→32[1][3]. 3) **Edge cases**: Test 800x600 low-res (should use 32px not scaled)[3], multiple monitors different DPIs. 4) **Clean install test**: Delete %APPDATA%/Ferrite, fresh install → verify Start Menu icon immediately crisp. 5) **Checksum**: Generate SHA256 of release .exe pre/post-fix for v0.2.6 release notes.

tasks.title: Fix Scroll Sync Using Viewport-Based Line Detection
tasks.description: Replace the failed mathematical position calculation in scroll synchronization with viewport-based LineMapping detection for accurate bidirectional Editor-Preview scroll sync.
tasks.details:
Implement in src/preview/sync_scroll.rs within the ScrollSync struct:

1. **Verify LineMapping properties**: Confirm LineMapping.rendered_y is content-relative (0 at document top), rendered_height is block height, start_line/end_line map to source lines. Focus on top-level blocks only (no sub-elements unless alignment issues persist).

2. **Editor -> Preview sync**:
   - From FerriteEditor::ViewState, get first_visible_line.
   - Binary search LineMapping vec to find mapping where start_line <= first_visible_line <= end_line.
   - Set preview ScrollArea scroll_offset_y = found_mapping.rendered_y.

3. **Preview -> Editor sync**:
   - From preview ScrollArea, get current scroll_offset.
   - Binary search LineMapping to find first mapping where mapping.rendered_y + rendered_height > scroll_offset.
   - Set FerriteEditor first_visible_line = found_mapping.start_line.

4. **LineMapping access**: Ensure LineCache exposes current LineMapping vec (rebuild on content change). Use Vec<LineMapping> { rendered_y: f32, rendered_height: f32, start_line: usize, end_line: usize }.

5. **Debounce & direction detection**: Add 16ms debounce, detect scroll direction to avoid ping-pong oscillation. Consider egui visibility_list for optimization but prioritize LineMapping approach.

6. **Edge cases**: Handle empty document, single-line files, scroll to exact top/bottom, partial block visibility (use block top for alignment).

Replace all existing mathematical percentage calculations with this mapping-based approach.
tasks.testStrategy:
1) Load 1MB markdown with mixed content (paragraphs, code blocks, lists, tables). Verify bidirectional scroll sync: editor scroll → preview follows instantly (<50ms), preview scroll → editor follows. Alignment: source line N aligns with corresponding preview block top.
2) Test edge cases: scroll to document top/bottom, single-line file, scroll mid-block (verify jumps to block start_line).
3) Performance: scroll 4MB file continuously for 30s, verify <5% CPU, no lag/stutter.
4) Regression: verify Task 29 basic sync still works, no breaking changes to EditorWidget.
5) Debug logging: add temporary logs showing 'Editor first_visible_line=X → Preview rendered_y=Y', verify mappings correct.

tasks.title: Fix Outline/Minimap Cursor Placement Off by Several Lines
tasks.description: When clicking a heading in the outline or minimap, the cursor is placed incorrectly - several lines below the intended target. The detection back to minimap works correctly; only the initial cursor placement from click is off.
tasks.details:
Bug details: Clicking 'Installation' in outline places cursor at 'Pre-built Binaries' (3 lines below). This happens with word wrap disabled, so it's not wrap-related. The cursor position IS correctly detected back to the minimap/outline after placement - only the initial go-to-line from click is wrong.

Investigate:
1. Outline click handler in src/ui/outline.rs - check how line number is calculated from heading
2. FerriteEditor scroll_to_line / set_cursor_line methods
3. Check if there's an off-by-N error in line indexing (0-indexed vs 1-indexed)
4. Check if heading line numbers stored in outline are stale after edits
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Open README.md, click each heading in outline - cursor should land on that exact heading line. 2) Test with word wrap on/off. 3) Test after editing document (add/remove lines) - outline should update. 4) Test minimap clicks similarly.

tasks.title: Add .txt to Default File Type Filter in Open Dialog
tasks.description: Add Text Files (*.txt) to the default visible file types in the Open File dialog, so users don't need to switch to 'All Files' to see .txt files.
tasks.details:
Currently the Open File dialog shows: Markdown, JSON, YAML, TOML files by default. Users must change to 'Text Files' or 'All Files' to see .txt files.

Implementation:
1. Find file dialog setup code (likely in src/app.rs or src/ui/)
2. Add 'Text Files (*.txt;*.text)' to the filter list
3. Consider making it part of the first/default filter group

Note: rfd (Rust File Dialog) crate is likely used - check its filter API.
tasks.testStrategy: 1) File > Open - verify .txt files visible without changing filter. 2) Verify other file types (md, json, yaml, toml) still visible. 3) Test on Windows and Linux.

tasks.title: Fix Text Jumping to Next Line When Typing at End of Line
tasks.description: In raw editor mode, placing cursor at end of a line and typing causes text and cursor to unexpectedly jump to the next line. Text should stay on same line until Enter is pressed.
tasks.details:
Bug reproduction:
1. Open any file in raw mode
2. Place cursor at the end of any line
3. Type any character
4. BUG: Text and cursor jump to next line

Expected: Text stays on current line, only wraps visually if word wrap is on.

Investigate in src/editor/ferrite/:
- input/keyboard.rs - character insertion handling
- editor.rs - insert_char method
- Check if there's an off-by-one in cursor position after insert
- Check if newline is being inadvertently inserted
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Type at end of multiple lines - no jumping. 2) Type in middle of line - no jumping. 3) Test with word wrap on/off. 4) Test with different line endings (LF, CRLF).

tasks.title: Fix Cannot Scroll to Bottom of Large Files - Missing Lines
tasks.description: Large files cannot scroll to show the last lines. Without word wrap, the last line is missing. With word wrap, even more lines are missing at the bottom.
tasks.details:
Bug details:
- Without word wrap: Last ~1 line not visible when scrolled to bottom
- With word wrap: Multiple lines missing at bottom

Root cause likely in ViewState scroll bounds calculation:
1. Check src/editor/ferrite/view.rs - total_content_height calculation
2. Verify scroll_max accounts for all lines including wrapped content
3. Check if viewport height is subtracted correctly
4. May need to add extra padding at bottom for last line visibility

Related: Virtual scrolling may be cutting off content prematurely.
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Open 10K+ line file, scroll to bottom - last line fully visible. 2) Enable word wrap, scroll to bottom - all wrapped content visible. 3) Test at different zoom levels. 4) Test with/without line numbers.

tasks.title: Add Document Navigation Buttons (Top/Middle/Bottom)
tasks.description: Add subtle navigation buttons to raw and rendered editor modes for instant jumping to top, middle, or bottom of the document.
tasks.details:
Feature specification:
- Position: Top-left corner of editor area (subtle, semi-transparent when not hovered)
- Buttons: Top (↑ or ⤒), Middle (⬌ or ◉), Bottom (↓ or ⤓)
- Behavior:
  - Top: Scroll to line 1, cursor at start
  - Middle: Scroll to line_count/2, cursor at that line
  - Bottom: Scroll to last line, cursor at end
- Should work in both raw editor (FerriteEditor) and rendered preview (MarkdownEditor)
- Consider keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+Home, Ctrl+End (if not already bound)

Implementation:
1. Add overlay buttons in EditorWidget and MarkdownEditor
2. Use egui::Area for floating overlay
3. Style to match theme (dark/light)
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Click each button - jumps to correct position. 2) Test in raw and rendered modes. 3) Test with various file sizes. 4) Verify buttons don't obstruct content. 5) Test hover states and theme compatibility.

tasks.title: Fix File Browser Context Menu Icon Rendering
tasks.description: Investigate and fix the issue where file browser context menu items (Rename, Delete, Reveal in Explorer, Refresh) show doubled or square/fallback icons instead of the expected symbols.
tasks.details:
Goal: Ensure the file browser’s right-click context menu renders the correct single icon per action, using a consistent icon source and size so icons never appear doubled or as empty/square glyphs.

Scope:
- Affects the file browser panel’s context menu when right-clicking on files and folders.
- Menu entries include at least: Rename, Delete, Reveal in Explorer, Refresh (extend fix to any other entries using the same icon code path).

Implementation plan:
1. **Locate context menu implementation**
   - Find the file browser UI module (e.g., `src/ui/file_browser.rs` or similar) where the right-click context menu is built.
   - Identify the code path that:
     - Creates the context menu items.
     - Attaches icons to each item (icon font, SVG, or image-based).

2. **Analyze current icon rendering logic**
   - Determine how icons are currently provided:
     - Are they using an icon font (e.g., specific Unicode code points)?
     - Are they using egui built-in icons, custom textures, or images?
   - Inspect if each menu entry is:
     - Rendering the icon twice (e.g., via both a `Label` with an icon glyph and an `Image`/`RichText` icon), or
     - Falling back to a missing-glyph square (e.g., using a codepoint not supported by the current font).
   - Check for style or layout helpers that might implicitly add an icon (e.g., wrappers that prepend icons automatically) resulting in duplication.

3. **Normalize icon source and mapping**
   - Define a **single, consistent icon source** for the file browser context menu (for example, a small internal enum mapping actions to icon IDs or codepoints):
     ```rust
     enum FileContextIcon {
         Rename,
         Delete,
         RevealInExplorer,
         Refresh,
     }

     impl FileContextIcon {
         fn as_char(&self) -> char {
             match self {
                 FileContextIcon::Rename => '\u{E100}', // example
                 FileContextIcon::Delete => '\u{E101}',
                 FileContextIcon::RevealInExplorer => '\u{E102}',
                 FileContextIcon::Refresh => '\u{E103}',
             }
         }
     }
     ```
   - Ensure all context menu items use **only this icon mapping**, not ad‑hoc codepoints or duplicated widgets.

4. **Fix double rendering**
   - Audit each menu item’s construction:
     - If an item is created by e.g. `ui.button(RichText::new("🗑 Delete"))` and then wrapped by another helper that also supplies an icon, refactor to one source of truth.
     - Prefer a dedicated helper like:
       ```rust
       fn file_menu_item(ui: &mut egui::Ui, icon: FileContextIcon, label: &str) -> egui::Response {
           let text = egui::RichText::new(format!("{}  {}", icon.as_char(), label));
           ui.button(text)
       }
       ```
     - Remove any extra `Image`, `Icon`, or label calls that would cause a second visual icon.

5. **Fix square / fallback glyphs**
   - Confirm that the font used by the file browser UI includes the chosen icon glyphs.
     - If it does not, either:
       - Switch to codepoints known to be supported by the existing icon font, or
       - Register an appropriate icon font in the global egui font definitions and ensure the context menu text style uses it.
   - Verify the correct text style is applied to menu labels (e.g., using a style that routes to the icon font where needed instead of the default body font if that lacks icons).

6. **Align with FerriteEditor rendering stack**
   - Reuse existing egui configuration from FerriteEditor (Task 7 and Task 6) for consistent icon scaling and DPI behavior where appropriate (e.g., use the same `Ui` style or layout conventions so icons look crisp and aligned with text).
   - Ensure any changes to fonts or styles do not negatively affect editor text rendering (FerriteEditor’s text galleys and line cache from Tasks 5, 6, 7 should remain unaffected by UI-only icon changes).

7. **Layout and sizing considerations**
   - Ensure icons and labels are aligned on a single baseline and use consistent spacing (e.g., small fixed horizontal padding between icon and label).
   - Confirm icon size is appropriate for typical DPI/scale factors and does not cause menu row height to deviate unexpectedly from other menus in the app.

8. **Guard against regressions**
   - Encapsulate icon creation for context menus in a small helper module or function so future menu items reuse the same pattern and cannot easily regress to doubled or unsupported icons.
   - Add inline comments describing which font/codepoints are used and why, to help future maintenance.

Deliverables:
- Updated file browser context menu code with a unified icon rendering approach.
- Optional small helper for context menu item creation to ensure consistency.
- Brief note in code comments or internal docs describing icon mapping and font assumptions.
tasks.testStrategy:
Manual and exploratory tests:
1. **Basic visual verification**
   - Open the file browser panel.
   - Right-click on a regular file.
   - In the context menu, verify that each of: Rename, Delete, Reveal in Explorer, Refresh displays **exactly one** icon, no duplicates.
   - Confirm none of the icons appear as generic squares, tofu boxes, or missing-glyph symbols.

2. **File vs folder vs empty area**
   - Right-click on a folder and verify icons still render correctly for all applicable menu items.
   - Right-click on an empty area in the file browser and confirm that any menu items present either:
     - Render icons correctly, or
     - Intentionally have no icons (and do not show squares).

3. **Theme and DPI checks**
   - Switch between available app themes (light/dark or others) and verify icons remain visible, correctly colored, and not doubled.
   - On a high-DPI display or with OS scaling > 100%, verify icons remain sharp, correctly aligned with labels, and still not doubled.

4. **Regression check with editor UI**
   - Open FerriteEditor with a large file (e.g., 4MB) to ensure the editor still renders text correctly (leveraging existing rendering from Tasks 6 and 7).
   - Confirm no changes to fonts or styles have caused editor text to display incorrect glyphs or squares.

5. **Stress / multi-open test**
   - Rapidly open and close the file browser context menu on various files and folders (20–30 times) and visually confirm icons remain consistent, without flicker, duplication, or random fallback squares.

6. **Code inspection / consistency check**
   - Review the context menu construction code to ensure all file browser menu items use the same icon helper or mapping and that there is only one icon source per menu item.

tasks.title: Change Link Behavior in Rendered Mode - Remove Gear Icon
tasks.description: In rendered/preview mode, remove the gear icon that appears on hover over links. Instead, make clicking the link text open edit mode (like gear did), and Ctrl+Click to open in browser.
tasks.details:
Current behavior:
- Hovering over a link shows a gear icon
- Clicking the gear opens edit mode for the link
- This is not intuitive UX

Desired behavior:
- No gear icon on hover
- Single click on link text: Opens edit mode (inline editing of the link)
- Ctrl+Click on link text: Opens URL in default browser

Implementation:
1. Find link rendering code in `src/markdown/editor.rs` or `src/markdown/widgets.rs`
2. Remove gear icon overlay on hover
3. Add click handler: check for Ctrl modifier
   - If Ctrl held: open URL in browser using `open::that(url)`
   - If no Ctrl: trigger edit mode for the link
4. Update cursor to indicate clickable (hand cursor on hover)
5. Consider adding tooltip on hover showing the URL and hint about Ctrl+Click
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Hover over link - no gear icon should appear. 2) Click link - should enter edit mode for that link. 3) Ctrl+Click link - should open in default browser. 4) Test with both internal (#anchor) and external (http://) links. 5) Verify cursor changes to hand on hover.

tasks.title: Fix Initial Cursor Position and Cursor Appearance
tasks.description: When opening a new document, cursor should be ready to type without clicking. Also fix cursor appearance: currently a static red line that's too tall, should be blinking and properly sized.
tasks.details:
Issues to fix:

1. **Initial cursor focus**
   - New documents should auto-focus the editor
   - Cursor should be at position (0,0) ready to type
   - Currently requires clicking to activate

2. **Cursor appearance**
   - Current: Static red line, no blinking, appears too tall
   - Desired: Proper blinking cursor (standard ~500ms on/off)
   - Height should match line height, not exceed it
   - Color should match theme (not always red)

Implementation:
1. In `src/editor/ferrite/editor.rs`:
   - Add blink state: `cursor_blink_timer: f32`, `cursor_visible: bool`
   - In render loop: toggle visibility every 500ms using `ctx.request_repaint_after()`
   - Only render cursor rect when `cursor_visible` is true

2. For cursor height:
   - Use actual line height from galley, not hardcoded value
   - Cursor rect height = line_height (not line_height + padding)

3. For initial focus:
   - Check `Tab.needs_focus` flag in `src/state.rs`
   - Call `response.request_focus()` when flag is set
   - Clear flag after focusing
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Create new document - cursor should blink immediately without clicking. 2) Cursor should blink at ~500ms intervals. 3) Cursor height should match text line height. 4) Cursor color should match theme. 5) Test focus after switching tabs.

tasks.title: Fix Text Selection Visibility - Make Selection Semi-Transparent
tasks.description: When selecting text, the selection highlight is completely opaque making it impossible to see the selected text. Make selection semi-transparent so text remains readable.
tasks.details:
Current issue:
- Text selection uses solid/opaque background color
- Selected text is completely hidden behind the selection
- User cannot see what they have selected

Desired behavior:
- Selection background should be semi-transparent (alpha ~40-60%)
- Selected text should remain clearly visible
- Selection color should contrast with both light and dark themes

Implementation:
1. Find selection rendering in `src/editor/ferrite/selection.rs`
2. Locate the `painter.rect_filled()` call for selection background
3. Change the color to use alpha channel:
   ```rust
   // Instead of solid color
   let selection_color = Color32::from_rgba_unmultiplied(100, 150, 255, 100); // ~40% alpha
   ```
4. Ensure selection color works in both light and dark themes
5. Test contrast - selected text should be clearly readable

Alternative approach:
- Use `painter.rect_filled()` with blend mode if available
- Or render selection BEFORE text so text draws on top
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Select text - must be able to read selected text through highlight. 2) Test in dark theme - selection visible and text readable. 3) Test in light theme - same verification. 4) Select across multiple lines - all text readable. 5) Compare alpha values to find optimal visibility.

tasks.title: Fix Tab Key Loses Focus Instead of Inserting Tab
tasks.description: Pressing Tab in FerriteEditor loses focus to another egui component instead of inserting a tab character or spaces. Need to consume the Tab key event.
tasks.details:
Bug: Tab key cycles focus to other UI elements instead of inserting tab/spaces in editor.

Root cause: Tab is an egui navigation key and is not being consumed by FerriteEditor.

Fix:
1. In `src/editor/ferrite/editor.rs` ui() method, add Tab key handling
2. Check for Key::Tab event and consume it
3. Insert tab character or spaces based on settings (tab_size)
4. Prevent event from propagating to egui focus system
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Press Tab in editor - should insert tab/spaces. 2) Focus should stay in editor. 3) Shift+Tab for dedent if implemented.

tasks.title: Optimize Large File Performance (500k+ lines)
tasks.description: 500,000 line (84MB) file uses 368MB RAM and is laggy, while 36,000 line file works flawlessly. Investigate and optimize for very large files.
tasks.details:
Issue: 84MB file uses 368MB RAM (4.4x file size) and is laggy.
Expected: Should use ~100-150MB RAM based on architecture goals.

Investigate:
1. Line cache memory usage for 500k lines
2. Check if any O(N) operations run per-frame
3. Profile with cargo flamegraph if available
4. Compare memory profile between 36k and 500k line files
5. Check if syntax highlighting scales poorly
6. Review LineCache eviction policy for very large files

Potential fixes:
- Reduce line cache size for huge files
- More aggressive galley eviction
- Lazy syntax highlighting (only visible + small buffer)
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Load 500k line file - should use <200MB RAM. 2) Scrolling should be smooth (60fps). 3) Typing should have no perceptible lag.

tasks.title: Fix Scroll Event Propagation Between Panels
tasks.description: When cursor is active in raw view, scrolling in workspace folder or rendered preview also scrolls the raw editor. Scroll events should be isolated to the panel being scrolled.
tasks.details:
Bug: With cursor active in raw editor:
- Scrolling in workspace folder tree → also scrolls raw editor
- Scrolling in rendered preview pane → also scrolls raw editor

Expected: Each panel should handle its own scroll events independently.

Root cause: Scroll events may be propagating through egui, or the editor is capturing scroll events globally.

Investigate:
1. Check FerriteEditor scroll handling in ui()
2. Verify egui Sense configuration for scroll areas
3. Check if response.hovered() is being used to gate scroll handling
4. May need to check if mouse is over editor rect before processing scroll
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Click in raw editor, scroll in file tree - editor should not scroll. 2) Click in raw editor, scroll in preview - editor should not scroll. 3) Scroll in editor - should scroll editor only.

tasks.title: Add Horizontal Scrollbar for Long Lines
tasks.description: When word wrap is off and lines extend beyond viewport, no horizontal scrollbar appears. Need to add scrollbar for horizontal navigation.
tasks.details:
Issue: With word wrap disabled, long lines extend beyond visible area but there's no scrollbar to navigate.

Implementation:
1. In FerriteEditor, track max_line_width from visible lines
2. Compare to viewport width
3. If content wider than viewport, render horizontal scrollbar
4. Scrollbar should control horizontal_scroll offset in ViewState
5. Scrollbar thumb size = viewport_width / max_content_width
6. Allow click-drag and scroll wheel (with shift?) for horizontal scroll
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Open file with long lines, word wrap OFF. 2) Horizontal scrollbar should appear. 3) Dragging scrollbar should scroll content. 4) Clicking on scrollbar track should jump.

tasks.title: Fix F3 Find Next Shortcut Not Working
tasks.description: F3 does not work as Find Next match shortcut, but Enter does in the find panel. Need to ensure F3 works globally when find panel has matches.
tasks.details:
Issue: F3 shortcut for Find Next doesn't work, though Enter in find panel does.

Investigate:
1. Check if F3 event reaches handle_keyboard_shortcuts()
2. Check if FerriteEditor is consuming F3 event
3. Verify handle_find_next() is being called
4. handle_find_next() returns early if find panel not visible - may need to change this

Fix options:
1. Ensure F3 propagates to global handler
2. Or handle F3 directly in FerriteEditor and trigger find_next
3. Consider: F3 should work even after closing find panel if matches exist
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Open find panel, search for text. 2) Press F3 - should go to next match. 3) Shift+F3 - should go to previous match. 4) F3 with cursor in editor (not find box) should still work.

tasks.title: Fix Syntax Highlighting Theme Switching
tasks.description: Changing syntax highlighting theme in settings does nothing. Normal syntax highlighting works, but theme changes don't apply.
tasks.details:
Issue: Syntax highlighting themes (25+ available) don't change the appearance when switched.

Investigate:
1. Find syntax theme selection in settings UI
2. Trace how theme is passed to syntax highlighter
3. Check if LineCache is invalidated on theme change
4. Verify theme hash is used in cache key
5. Check if FerriteEditor receives theme change notification

Fix:
- Ensure theme change invalidates line cache
- Pass current syntax theme to highlighter
- May need to store theme in FerriteEditor and compare for changes
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Open syntax-highlighted file (.rs, .py, etc). 2) Change syntax theme in settings. 3) Colors should update immediately. 4) Test multiple theme switches.

tasks.title: Fix Code Folding Folding Too Much Content
tasks.description: Code folding sometimes folds the entire document or too many lines when clicking a fold indicator that should only fold a small region.
tasks.details:
Issue: Clicking fold indicator on a heading or code block folds way more content than expected, sometimes the entire document.

Investigate:
1. Check fold region detection in `src/editor/folding.rs`
2. Verify fold end detection logic
3. Check if fold regions are being calculated correctly for markdown
4. Heading folds should stop at next heading of same or higher level
5. Code block folds should only fold the code block

Fix:
- Review fold boundary detection algorithm
- Add better end-of-fold detection for markdown structures
- May need to track heading hierarchy
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Fold a ## heading - should fold until next ## or # heading. 2) Fold a code block - should only fold that block. 3) Fold a list - should only fold list items.

tasks.title: Connect Auto-Close Brackets to FerriteEditor
tasks.description: Auto-close brackets setting exists but doesn't work in FerriteEditor. The feature needs to be connected to the new editor.
tasks.details:
Issue: auto_close_brackets setting exists but typing ( doesn't insert ).

The auto-close logic exists in app.rs but uses Tab.content and Tab.cursors which aren't synced with FerriteEditor.

Fix options:
1. Implement auto-close directly in FerriteEditor's text input handling
2. When typing opener character, also insert closer and keep cursor between
3. Handle: (), [], {}, '', "", ``
4. Smart handling: don't auto-close after alphanumeric (for contractions like "don't")

Implementation in editor.rs:
- After inserting opener, check if auto-close enabled
- Insert matching closer
- Don't move cursor (it stays between the pair)
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Enable auto-close brackets in settings. 2) Type ( - should become (). 3) Type [ - should become []. 4) Type ' after letter - should NOT auto-close. 5) Type " at word start - should auto-close.

tasks.title: Fix UTF-16BE File Display Issues
tasks.description: UTF-16BE encoded files show squares and garbled characters while other encodings (UTF-8, UTF-16LE, Latin-1, etc.) work correctly.
tasks.details:
Issue: UTF-16BE files display incorrectly with square boxes and wrong characters.
Other encodings work: UTF-8, UTF-16LE, Latin-1, Shift-JIS, etc.

Investigate:
1. Check encoding detection in file loading code
2. Verify BOM detection for UTF-16BE (FE FF)
3. Check byte order handling in conversion
4. Compare UTF-16LE (works) vs UTF-16BE (broken) code paths
5. Test file: test_md/encoding_tests/utf16be.txt

Fix:
- Ensure proper big-endian byte swapping
- Verify encoding_rs or similar handles UTF-16BE correctly
- May need explicit BOM check and handling
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Open test_md/encoding_tests/utf16be.txt. 2) Content should display correctly (no squares). 3) Edit and save - encoding preserved. 4) Compare with utf16le.txt display.

tasks.title: Fix Ctrl+G Go to Line Not Working
tasks.description: Ctrl+G keyboard shortcut for Go to Line dialog doesn't work. Need to verify shortcut is registered and dialog appears.
tasks.details:
Issue: Pressing Ctrl+G does nothing, should open a Go to Line dialog.

Investigate:
1. Check if GoToLine shortcut is registered in settings.rs
2. Check if KeyboardAction::GoToLine is handled
3. Verify the Go to Line dialog exists and is wired up
4. May need to implement the dialog if missing

Dialog should:
- Open small input dialog
- Accept line number
- Navigate to that line on Enter
- Handle invalid input gracefully (out of range, non-numeric)
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Press Ctrl+G - dialog should appear. 2) Enter line number - should navigate to that line. 3) Enter invalid number - should show error or clamp. 4) Escape should close dialog.

tasks.title: Fix Scroll to Bottom Missing Lines (Task 40 Regression)
tasks.description: Cannot scroll to show the last lines of a document. Scrollbar reaches bottom but content is cut off. This was addressed in Task 40 but may have regressed.
tasks.details:
Issue: When scrolling to the bottom of a document, the last several lines are not visible even though the scrollbar is at the bottom position.

This was supposedly fixed in Task 40 but appears to have regressed or wasn't fully fixed.

Investigate:
1. Review Task 40 fix in ViewState
2. Check total_content_height calculation
3. Verify scroll_max allows viewing last line
4. Check if word wrap affects bottom padding differently
5. May need extra viewport padding at bottom

The fix should ensure:
- Last line is fully visible when scrolled to bottom
- Works with word wrap on and off
- Works with different line heights
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Open large file, scroll to bottom - last line fully visible. 2) Enable word wrap, scroll to bottom - all content visible. 3) Test with various file sizes.
